message:
feat(packs): integrate external pack loading into test command evaluation

Extends the test command to evaluate external YAML packs loaded from
custom_paths config, enabling users to define custom rule packs outside
the built-in registry.

Changes:
- Load external packs via load_external_packs() from expanded custom_paths
- Auto-enable all external pack IDs alongside built-in enabled packs
- Merge external pack keywords into keyword set for quick rejection
- After built-in evaluation allows command, check external packs for deny
- Construct PatternMatch result from external pack match details

This completes the external pack loading pipeline: config → glob expansion
→ YAML parsing → runtime evaluation. Users can now add custom rules by
placing YAML files in their custom_paths directories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-v2ti","title":"[E0-T1] Create comprehensive E2E test infrastructure","description":"## Task: Create Comprehensive E2E Test Infrastructure

### Context
This is a CROSS-CUTTING task that provides the foundation for E2E testing across ALL 10 epics. Each epic's testing task will depend on this infrastructure.

### What To Do
Create a robust E2E test framework with detailed logging, fixture management, and reusable test utilities.

### Implementation Details

1. **Test Framework Structure:**
   ```
   tests/
   ├── e2e/
   │   ├── mod.rs                    # E2E test module
   │   ├── framework.rs              # Test framework utilities
   │   ├── logging.rs                # Detailed test logging
   │   ├── fixtures/
   │   │   ├── configs/              # Test config files
   │   │   │   ├── minimal.toml
   │   │   │   ├── full_featured.toml
   │   │   │   ├── path_specific.toml
   │   │   │   ├── temporary_rules.toml
   │   │   │   └── agent_profiles.toml
   │   │   ├── commands/             # Test command sets
   │   │   │   ├── dangerous.txt
   │   │   │   ├── safe.txt
   │   │   │   └── edge_cases.txt
   │   │   └── git_repos/            # Mock git repos for testing
   │   ├── test_pattern_testing.rs   # Epic 1 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_explanations.rs      # Epic 2 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_suggestions.rs       # Epic 3 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_auto_suggest.rs      # Epic 4 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_path_allowlist.rs    # Epic 5 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_temp_allowlist.rs    # Epic 6 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_interactive.rs       # Epic 7 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_git_awareness.rs     # Epic 8 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_agent_profiles.rs    # Epic 9 E2E tests
   │   └── test_graduated_response.rs # Epic 10 E2E tests
   └── scripts/
       ├── run_e2e_tests.sh          # Master E2E test runner
       ├── setup_test_env.sh         # Environment setup
       └── cleanup_test_env.sh       # Cleanup
   ```

2. **Test Framework Core (framework.rs):**
   ```rust
   pub struct E2ETestContext {
       pub temp_dir: TempDir,
       pub config_path: PathBuf,
       pub history_db: PathBuf,
       pub log_file: PathBuf,
       pub env_vars: HashMap<String, String>,
   }

   impl E2ETestContext {
       pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_config(self, config: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_git_repo(self, branch: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_agent(self, agent: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn run_dcg(&self, args: &[&str]) -> DcgOutput { ... }
       pub fn assert_blocked(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn assert_allowed(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn cleanup(self) { ... }
   }

   pub struct DcgOutput {
       pub stdout: String,
       pub stderr: String,
       pub exit_code: i32,
       pub duration_ms: u64,
   }
   ```

3. **Detailed Logging (logging.rs):**
   ```rust
   pub struct TestLogger {
       log_path: PathBuf,
       verbose: bool,
   }

   impl TestLogger {
       pub fn log_test_start(&self, name: &str, description: &str) { ... }
       pub fn log_step(&self, step: &str, details: &str) { ... }
       pub fn log_command(&self, cmd: &str, args: &[&str]) { ... }
       pub fn log_output(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn log_assertion(&self, assertion: &str, passed: bool) { ... }
       pub fn log_test_end(&self, name: &str, result: TestResult) { ... }
       pub fn generate_report(&self) -> TestReport { ... }
   }
   ```

4. **E2E Test Script (run_e2e_tests.sh):**
   ```bash
   #!/bin/bash
   set -euo pipefail

   LOG_DIR="./test_logs/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
   mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

   echo "=== DCG E2E Test Suite ===" | tee "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"

   # Build release binary
   cargo build --release 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/build.log"

   # Run each test category
   for test in pattern_testing explanations suggestions auto_suggest \
               path_allowlist temp_allowlist interactive git_awareness \
               agent_profiles graduated_response; do
       echo "Running: test_${test}..." | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
       cargo test --test "test_${test}" -- --nocapture 2>&1 | \
           tee "$LOG_DIR/${test}.log"
       echo "  Result: $?" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   done

   echo "Completed: $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   echo "Logs saved to: $LOG_DIR"
   ```

### Fixture Requirements

1. **Config Fixtures:**
   - minimal.toml: Bare minimum config
   - full_featured.toml: All features enabled
   - path_specific.toml: Path-aware allowlists
   - temporary_rules.toml: TTL and session rules
   - agent_profiles.toml: Per-agent settings
   - git_awareness.toml: Branch-specific settings
   - graduated_response.toml: Response graduation config

2. **Command Fixtures:**
   - dangerous.txt: Commands that should be blocked
   - safe.txt: Commands that should be allowed
   - edge_cases.txt: Boundary conditions, unicode, special chars
   - heredocs.txt: Multiline/heredoc commands

3. **Mock Git Repos:**
   - Script to create temp git repos with specific branches
   - main, master, feature/*, release/* branches

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Test framework compiles and basic tests pass
- [ ] All fixture files created and validated
- [ ] run_e2e_tests.sh runs successfully
- [ ] Log files contain detailed, parseable output
- [ ] Test report generation works

### Dependencies
- None - this is foundational infrastructure

### Files to Create
- tests/e2e/mod.rs
- tests/e2e/framework.rs
- tests/e2e/logging.rs
- tests/e2e/fixtures/* (all fixture files)
- tests/scripts/run_e2e_tests.sh
- tests/scripts/setup_test_env.sh","notes":"Created comprehensive E2E test infrastructure:
- tests/e2e/mod.rs, framework.rs, logging.rs - Rust test modules
- 7 config fixtures in tests/e2e/fixtures/configs/
- 4 command fixtures in tests/e2e/fixtures/commands/
- tests/scripts/run_e2e_tests.sh - Master E2E runner
- tests/scripts/setup_test_env.sh - Environment setup
All files syntactically correct.  {"id":"git_safety_guard-v47","title":"[PACK] secrets.aws_secrets - AWS Secrets Manager Pack","description":"# AWS Secrets Manager Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS Secrets Manager and Parameter Store operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Secrets Manager
- `aws secretsmanager delete-secret`
- `aws secretsmanager delete-resource-policy`
- `aws secretsmanager remove-regions-from-replication`

### Parameter Store (SSM)
- `aws ssm delete-parameter`
- `aws ssm delete-parameters`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`
- `aws secretsmanager describe-secret`
- `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value`
- `aws ssm get-parameter`, `get-parameters`
- `aws ssm describe-parameters`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: aws, secretsmanager, ssm
- Note: delete-secret has recovery window by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Secrets Manager patterns covered
- [ ] Parameter Store patterns covered
- [ ] Tests verify common operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:31:01.807224465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:34:47.819316228Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:34:47.819316228Z","close_reason":"Completed: added secrets.aws_secrets pack + tests and registered in pack registry.  {"id":"git_safety_guard-v5d9","title":"[E9-T5] Add detected agent to verbose output","description":"## Task: Add detected agent to verbose output

### Parent Epic
Epic 9: Agent-Specific Profiles (git_safety_guard-pwsr)

### What To Do
Show which agent was detected in verbose and debug output.

### Implementation Details
1. Verbose output:
   ```
   $ dcg --verbose check "rm -rf /"
   [dcg] Detected agent: claude-code
   [dcg] Trust level: high
   [dcg] Effective packs: core, database, kubernetes
   [dcg] Allowlist entries: 5 (base) + 3 (agent) = 8
   
   🛑 BLOCKED...
   ```

2. Debug output:
   ```
   $ dcg --debug check "rm -rf /"
   [DEBUG] Agent detection:
     - CLAUDE_CODE env: set
     - CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env: set
     - Detected: ClaudeCode
     - Profile: agents.claude-code
   [DEBUG] Profile merge:
     - Base allowlist: 5 entries
     - Agent additional: 3 entries
     - Disabled packs: filesystem.temp_cleanup
   ```

3. JSON output (for test command):
   ```json
   {
     "agent": {
       "detected": "claude-code",
       "trust_level": "high",
       "detection_method": "environment_variable"
     },
     "outcome": "blocked",
     ...
   }
   ```

### Files to Modify
- src/cli.rs (verbose output)
- src/output/test.rs (JSON agent field)

### Dependencies
- E9-T1 (agent detection)
- E9-T3 (profile merging to report)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:47.306789752Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:28:15.598770172Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:28:15.598720358Z","close_reason":"Tasks completed successfully","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-v5d9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-cle3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:51.031876251Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-v5d9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-pwsr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:38:55.460419615Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-v5d9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-sb9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:51.094850362Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-v9nl","title":"[E8-T6] Graceful degradation when not in git repo","description":"## Task: Graceful degradation when not in git repo

### Parent Epic
Epic 8: Git Branch-Aware Strictness (git_safety_guard-1d6e)

### What To Do
Ensure dcg works correctly when not in a git repository.

### Implementation Details
1. Detection:
   ```rust
   fn is_git_repo() -> bool {
       std::path::Path::new(".git").exists() ||
       std::process::Command::new("git")
           .args(["rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree"])
           .output()
           .map(|o| o.status.success())
           .unwrap_or(false)
   }
   ```

2. Fallback behavior:
   - If not in git repo: use default strictness
   - If git command fails: use default strictness
   - No errors, just quiet fallback

3. Logging:
   - Debug log: "Not in git repository, using default strictness"
   - No user-facing warnings (unless verbose mode)

4. Configuration option:
   ```toml
   [git_awareness]
   warn_if_not_git = false  # Show warning if not in git repo
   ```

### Test Cases
- Run dcg in /tmp (no git)
- Run dcg in git repo
- Run dcg in subdirectory of git repo
- Run dcg with .git file (worktree)

### Files to Modify
- src/git.rs (fallback logic)
- src/evaluator.rs (handle None branch)

### Dependencies
- E8-T1 (detection functions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:39.194397499Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:53.362641941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-v9nl","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-p2ht","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:49.139791728Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","title":"[PACK] email.postmark - Postmark Pack","description":"# Postmark Email Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Postmark API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### API Operations
- Server deletion: `DELETE /servers/{id}`
- Template deletion: `DELETE /templates/{id}`
- Domain deletion/removal
- Sender signature deletion
- Webhook deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `GET /servers`
- `GET /templates`
- `GET /stats/outbound`
- Template listing
- Stats retrieval
- Email sending (POST)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: postmark
- Note: Known for transactional email reliability

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for server deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for template deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_postmark.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server deletion blocked
- [ ] Template deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:12:25.131023095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T09:57:16.572240238Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T09:57:16.572240238Z","close_reason":"Postmark pack complete: 7 destructive patterns (server, template, domain, sender signature, webhook, message stream, suppression deletion).  {"id":"git_safety_guard-vo1u","title":"[E2-T5] Write explanations for cloud packs (AWS/GCP/Azure)","description":"## Task: Write explanations for cloud packs

### Parent Epic
Epic 2: Natural Language Explanations (git_safety_guard-t01x)

### What To Do
Write explanations for AWS, GCP, and Azure pack patterns.

### AWS Patterns (examples)
1. ec2_terminate: "This command would permanently terminate EC2 instances. Any data on instance storage would be lost."

2. s3_rb_force: "This command would delete the S3 bucket and ALL its contents, including any objects and versions."

3. rds_delete: "This command would delete the RDS database instance. If skip-final-snapshot is used, all data is lost permanently."

4. iam_delete_user: "This command would delete the IAM user and their access keys, potentially breaking applications using those credentials."

### GCP Patterns (examples)
1. compute_delete: "This command would delete the Compute Engine instance and its boot disk."

2. storage_rm_r: "This command would recursively delete all objects in the bucket path."

### Azure Patterns (examples)
1. vm_delete: "This command would delete the VM and potentially its disks."

2. storage_delete: "This command would delete the storage account and all its contents."

### Quality Standards
- Note billing implications where relevant
- Emphasize cross-service dependencies
- Mention data retention/backup options

### Files to Modify
- src/packs/aws.rs
- src/packs/gcp.rs  
- src/packs/azure.rs

### Dependencies
- E2-T1 (explanation field must exist)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:06:39.256934673Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:26.621231592Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vo1u","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-q6f7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:36.384930350Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vo1u","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t01x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:38:55.318254409Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-vufo","title":"rm parser: quoted $TMPDIR only for combined flags (parity)","description":"Parser currently allows quoted $TMPDIR for separate/long flags; regex only allows quoted $TMPDIR for combined flags. Align parser with regex and add parity tests (deny rm -r -f "$TMPDIR/foo" and rm --recursive --force "$TMPDIR/foo"; keep combined flags allowed).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:40:36.162045081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:04:46.199678793Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:04:46.199678793Z","close_reason":"Align rm parser with regex: allow quoted  only for combined -rf; deny for separate/long flags; add parity tests; full gates + e2e green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vufo","author":"ubuntu","text":"Note: my close_reason string accidentally expanded $TMPDIR in the shell. Intended: allow double-quoted $TMPDIR only for combined -rf; deny quoted $TMPDIR for separate (-r -f) and long (--recursive --force) forms; parity tests added; gates+e2e green.","created_at":"2026-01-11T11:05:15Z"}]}
